channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Marvel Wants to Remind You ‘X-Men ’97’ Season 2 Is Really Close
item.description: Why not spend part of your summer reading the exploits of the 'X-Men '97' cast before the show returns in full?

item.title: New studies find Brits are the most anxious when it comes to robots — and they don't want data centers in their back yards either
item.description: UK adults show widespread unease around robots and local data centers, driven by security, environmental concerns, and limited firsthand exposure.

item.title: How to watch Oscars 2026 for *FREE* — stream 98th Academy Awards from anywhere
item.description: With Sinners and One Battle After Another set to win big at the 98th Academy Awards at the Dolby Theatre in LA, here's how to watch Oscars 2026 for free.

item.title: Aether OS is a computer in a browser built for the AT Protocol
item.description: Aether OS puts a full-fledged desktop in your browser that ties directly into the AT Protocol. That means it connects to your Bluesky account and other public records. It offers a pretty full suite of apps, 42 in total, covering text editing, task management, and social media. There's even a rudimentary tracker for making chiptunes, […]

item.title: Nvidia wants to have your cake and eat it: Jensen Huang describes the AI layered stack and hints at what world's most valuable firm will do next
item.description: Nvidia’s layered AI framework connects energy, chips, infrastructure, models, and applications, showing how real-time intelligence drives industrial expansion globally.

item.title: Test Glorious Model O Eternal : le retour aux sources à prix tout doux
item.description: La Glorious Model O Eternal est une version filaire, allégée et surtout accessible de la célèbre Model O qui a fait la réputation de la marque. Une base solide, pour un nouveau modèle de référence ?

item.title: ‘Voltron: Legendary Defender’ Forms a Physical Collection After Netflix Delisting
item.description: 'Voltron: Legendary Defender' may no longer be on Netflix, but in a few weeks, it can be with *you*...at least until you remember its ending.

item.title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
item.description: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

item.title: Arc Raiders replaced some of its AI-generated voice lines, using professional actors instead
item.description: In an unexpected twist, humans have taken some jobs back from AI. Embark Studios' CEO Patrick Söderlund recently told GamesIndustry.biz that the studio "re-recorded" some of the AI-generated voice lines in Arc Raiders with human voices, only after its successful launch in October. "There is a quality difference," Söderlund told GamesIndustry.biz. "A real professional actor is better than AI; that's just how it is." With Arc Raiders' player count peaking at nearly half a million users on Steam, the game's breakout success was still marred by its use of text-to-speech AI. While there was no gene...

item.title: Samsung is slashing prices on its best-rated TVs ahead of March Madness — here are 11 deals I'd buy from $249.99
item.description: Samsung is slashing prices on its best-rated 4K, QLED, and OLED TVs, and I'm hand-picking 11 TVs I'd recommend for watching March Madness.
